'''Ben Jones-Bishop''' (born 24 August 1988) is a [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er or {{rlp|fb}} for the [[York Knights]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].<ref name="England Knights v Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights v Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019}}</ref>

He played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Harlequins RL]] in the [[Super League|top flight]]. Jones-Bishop has also played for the [[Salford Red Devils]] and [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the Super League, and has played for the [[England Knights]] at international level.

==Background==
Jones-Bishop was born in [[Leeds]], [[West Yorkshire]], England. He is of Jamaican descent.<ref>{{Cite web | url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/england-knights-v-jamaica-how-the-two-teams-could-line-up-21242/ | title=England Knights v Jamaica: How the two teams could line-up | date=16 July 2019 }}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Leeds===
After impressing for the club's academy team, he made his début for the Leeds club in April 2008, in the victory over the [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]] in the [[Challenge Cup|Rugby League Challenge Cup]]. He was awarded the Man of the Match for his performance.{{Citation needed|date=November 2010}} He went on to make a further three appearances before going on loan to Harlequins for the 2010 season.{{Citation needed|date=November 2010}}

Jones-Bishop impressed during his season at Harlequins, scoring 11 tries in 18 games, before returning to the Leeds side for the 2011 season. Jones-Bishop scored a hat trick in Leeds' 32-28 Millennium Magic, scoring a penalty try, after being brought down off the ball by [[Gareth Raynor]] to give Leeds the win. He also won the man of the match award.

Jones-Bishop played on the wing and scored a try for Leeds in the [[2011 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Scott|first=Ged|title=Leeds 18-28 Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14670204|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=[[BBC Sport]]|date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news| url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/aug/27/leeds-rhinos-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup | location=London | work=The Guardian | first=Andy | last=Wilson | title=Leeds Rhinos 18-28 Wigan Warriors – Challenge Cup final match report | date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Brown|first=Oliver|title=Challenge Cup final: Leeds Rhinos 18 Wigan Warriors 28|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8727501/Challenge-Cup-final-Leeds-Rhinos-18-Wigan-Warriors-28.html|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=The Telegraph|date=27 August 2011}}</ref>

He finished off [[Super League XVI]] by playing on the wing in Leeds' 32-16 [[2011 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]] in [[Manchester]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-match-report?newsfeed=true|title=Leeds claim Grand Final glory as inspired Rob Burrow sinks St Helens|date=8 October 2011|work=Guardian|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8815944/St-Helens-16-Leeds-32-match-report.html|title=St Helens 16 Leeds 32|date=8 October 2011|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref>

He then was a late replacement on the wing for Leeds 26-12 victory in the [[2012 World Club Challenge]] win over [[National Rugby League]] premiers [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], scoring the winning try in the 76th minute off after good work by [[Rob Burrow]], and a [[Kallum Watkins]] pass that pushed Leeds' lead out to an unbeatable 20-12.

He played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref> He played in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web |date=6 October 2012 |title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final |access-date=20 November 2019 |publisher=Guardian}}</ref>

===Salford===
On 10 May 2014, Jones-Bishop announced he'd leave the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] club at the end of the season after rejecting a new deal to remain at the club. It was believed he was unhappy with the amount of playing time he had at the club. It was announced that he would join [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] for the 2015 season.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://superleague.co.uk/article/30175/jones-bishop-set-to-leave-rhinos |title=Jones-Bishop set to leave Rhinos |access-date=10 May 2014 |archive-date=12 May 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140512224306/http://superleague.co.uk/article/30175/jones-bishop-set-to-leave-rhinos |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12211/9304958/super-league-ben-jones-bishop-to-leave-leeds-rhinos-at-end-of-season |title=Super League: Ben Jones-Bishop to leave Leeds Rhinos at end of season | Leeds Rhinos Rugby News, Fixtures, Results | Sky Sports |access-date=10 May 2014 |archive-date=10 May 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140510211823/http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12211/9304958/super-league-ben-jones-bishop-to-leave-leeds-rhinos-at-end-of-season |url-status=dead }}</ref> He played 25 games for Salford in 2015 scoring 15 tries.

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 5 November 2015 it was announced that he would be joining [[Wakefield Trinity]] on a one-year deal in 2016.<ref>{{Cite web|title = Wakefield sign Ben Jones-Bishop from Salford|url = http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12208/10056419/wakefield-sign-ben-jones-bishop-from-salford|website = Sky Sports|access-date = 2016-02-13}}</ref>

===York City Knights===
On 26 January 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[York City Knights|York City]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=Loverugbyleague|date=26 Jan 2021|access-date=27 Jan 2021|title=York pull off Ben Jones-Bishop coup|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/york-pull-off-ben-jones-bishop-coup/|last1=Darbyshire |first1=Drew }}</ref>

===Sheffield Eagles===
On 17 November 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Sheffield Eagles|Sheffield]] in the RFL Championship.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total Rugby League|date=17 Nov 2021|accessdate=17 Nov 2021|title=Sheffield Eagles sign former Super League star from York City Knights|url=https://www.totalrl.com/sheffield-eagles-sign-former-super-league-star-from-york-city-knights/}}</ref>

===York Knights (re-join)===
On 5 March 2025 it was reported that he had rejoined [[York Knights]] in the RFL Championship.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=5 Mar 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Ben Jones-Bishop set for move to Championship rivals with Sheffield exit looming|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ben-jones-bishop-set-for-move-to-championship-rivals-with-sheffield-exit-looming}}</ref>

==International career==
In the second match of Jamaica's [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] campaign, Jones-Bishop scored Jamaica's first ever try at the tournament during their 68-6 loss to New Zealand at the [[MKM Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/63359346/|title=Rugby League World Cup: New Zealand 68-6 Jamaica - Dallin Watene-Zelezniak scores four tries|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
